# EB-5 Petition Package — {{PROJECT_LABEL}}

Prepared for role: **{{ROLE}}**  ·  Stage: **{{STAGE}}**  ·  Created: **{{CREATED}}**
Source-of-funds path(s) selected: **{{SOF_PATHS}}**

This is an organized, **privacy-safe** package for assembling an EB-5 (reserved category /
$800K) petition. The goal is simple: hand your immigration attorney a complete, well-labeled
set of documents so they spend their time filing — not chasing missing items.

> **Before anything else, read [`PRIVACY-AND-HANDLING.md`](./PRIVACY-AND-HANDLING.md).**
> The sensitive material (your IDs and source-of-funds proof) is added by **you**, locally.
> The tool that built this package never collects or stores those values.

## How to use this package

1. Work through **[`DOCUMENT_CHECKLIST.md`](./DOCUMENT_CHECKLIST.md)** top to bottom. Each
   item has a status box — mark it as you go.
2. Drop each real document into the matching numbered folder. Every folder has a `README.md`
   that says exactly what belongs there, what to name it, and the follow-up questions
   attorneys most commonly ask about it (answer those *up front*).
3. Fill the **templates** (`*_TEMPLATE.md`) — the source-of-funds narrative and the
   path-of-funds flow. These are the documents attorneys most often send back for rework, so
   they're worth getting right early. Save your filled copy **next to** the template.
4. Log every attorney question and your answer in
   `99_Correspondence_Log/Attorney_QA_Log.md` so nothing is asked twice.
5. When you think you're done, run a readiness check (see below) before sending.

## Folder map

| Folder | What it holds | Sensitivity |
|---|---|---|
| `01_Identity_and_Personal/` | Passport, photos, civil documents, prior US status | **High (PII)** |
| `02_Source_of_Funds/` | Proof of where the invested capital lawfully came from | **High** |
| `03_Path_of_Funds/` | The transfer trail from your account to the project | **High** |
| `04_Investment_Documents/` | Subscription, escrow, PPM, I-956F notice (from the issuer) | Medium |
| `05_Immigration_Forms/` | I-526E (and later I-829) forms and supporting exhibits | Medium |
| `06_Project_Due_Diligence/` | Independent vetting of the *project* (no personal data) | Low — shareable |
| `99_Correspondence_Log/` | Running Q&A / RFE log with your attorney | Low |

## Check your work (privacy-safe)

- **Find gaps:** run `/eb5-gapcheck "<path to this package>"`. It reports missing or
  incomplete artifacts and likely attorney follow-ups **by looking at structure only** — it
  does not open, read, or transmit your financial documents or PII.
- **Vet the project itself:** run `/eb5-vet "<project or regional center name>"` and save the
  resulting HTML report into `06_Project_Due_Diligence/`.

## Status legend (used throughout the checklist)

- `[ ]` not started · `[~]` in progress / partial · `[x]` complete and in the folder
- `[n/a]` does not apply to your situation (note why)

> General organizational guidance, **not legal advice**. Your own attorney determines what
> your specific filing requires.
